status_detail: Cycle 96 mistral envelope v7 RETEST WITH CACHE-BUSTER: BREAKTHROUGH DIAGNOSTIC EVIDENCE. Cache-buster strategy (URL ?cb=20260429140440 + DevTools Disable cache + ServiceWorker fresh) CONFIRMED v7 IS DEPLOYED at wire level — captured response body shows status='active' (not 'streamed'), title='⚠️ 보안 경고', content='⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.', AND contentChunks[0].text matches. Response size 1.7kB matches expected v7 size (1707B = v6 1620B + 87B content fill). #646's verdict that 'v7 didn't apply' was INCORRECT — that was cached evidence from v6. v7 IS deployed. HOWEVER end-to-end failure persists: identical TRPCClientError 'Unknown error' at e.from (cause/data/meta/shape ALL undefined) and bubble still does NOT render in DOM. Root cause hypothesis (HP-3 candidate): SuperJSON meta.values describes 'undefined' markers (e.g. messages[0].contentChunks[0]._context: ['undefined'], messages[0].agentId: ['undefined']) INCONSISTENT with actual payload values (those fields are null in JSON, not undefined). SuperJSON deserializer rejects this mismatch silently as 'Unknown error'. HP-2 (content fill at engine) was correct on wire side but INSUFFICIENT — need v8 envelope fixing meta.values to either omit those entries (let JSON null pass through) OR match actual JSON values consistently.
verdict_subagent: PARTIAL — major diagnostic breakthrough but bubble still not rendered
cache_strategy_used: disable_cache_only (DevTools Network Disable cache ☑️) + URL cache-buster + ServiceWorker fresh (incognito skipped because would force re-login)
auth_state: open_access (Sign in/Sign up shown in UI but chat fires anonymously and engine intercepts)
warning_text_observed: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (in response body content + contentChunks AND title='⚠️ 보안 경고', but NOT rendered to DOM bubble)
warning_text_match_in_envelope: literal '보안 경고' present in title; semantic block message in content
response_size_bytes: 1707 (matches v7 expected; +87B vs v6 1620B = content fill confirmed)
response_first_500_chars: [{"result":{"data":{"json":{"chatId":"43a3b643-40fd-4b06-b8d9-b27817ba978e","title":"⚠️ 보안 경고","messages":[{"id":"a8333d4b-cc87-4a33-aeaf-86d33c52b0f6","role":"assistant","content":"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.","contentChunks":[{"type":"text","text":"⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다.","_context":null}],"status":"active","generationStatus":"success","createdAt":"2026-04-27T00:00:00.000Z","files":[],"followups":[],...
content_field_value_observed: ⚠️ 민감정보가 포함된 요청은 보안 정책에 의해 차단되었습니다. (v7 fill — was '' in v6)
v7_deployment_evidence: All v7 markers present in wire-captured response: status='active', content has warning text, response size 1707B matches engine emit log size
TRPCClientError_evidence: Console: TRPCClientError 'Unknown error' at e.from. EXPANDED: name='TRPCClientError', message='Unknown error', cause=undefined, data=undefined, meta=undefined, shape=undefined — ALL fields undefined, suggesting SuperJSON deserialization throws inside transformer before tRPC error envelope can be constructed
progress_vs_646_baseline: new_evidence -- #646 verdict was incorrect (cached v6 state). #647 cache-buster reveals v7 IS deployed wire-level but TRPCClientError persists with identical signature
progress_vs_v5_baseline: regression at error layer (v5 had no TRPCClientError, v7 has Unknown error) but progress at content layer (v7 envelope contains warning text where v5 had empty content)
breakthrough_partial: diagnostic breakthrough (cache-buster method validated, v7 deployment confirmed, root cause narrowed to SuperJSON meta.values mismatch hypothesis HP-3)
root_cause_hypothesis_HP3: SuperJSON meta.values describes 'undefined' type markers for fields like messages[0].contentChunks[0]._context, messages[0].agentId, etc. — but actual JSON payload has those fields as null (not undefined). SuperJSON's null↔undefined transformer fails to reconcile → Unknown error in deserializer. v8 fix should either: (a) remove those entries from meta.values entirely (let JSON null pass through as null), OR (b) ensure JSON payload matches meta.values declarations consistently.
automation_validated: windows-mcp on user's real Chrome window worked perfectly. 7th consecutive successful run. Cache-buster strategy (URL+Disable cache+SW) effective for invalidating cached responses.
errors_observed: TRPCClientError: Unknown error at e.from — all error fields undefined (cause=undefined, data=undefined, meta=undefined, shape=undefined)

test_infra_findings.windows_mcp_validated_7th_time: Confirmed 7th consecutive successful run with windows-mcp on user's real Chrome
test_infra_findings.cache_buster_method_validated: URL cache-buster + DevTools Disable cache ☑️ + ServiceWorker fresh load = effective invalidation strategy. Reveals true wire response, not cached UI state.
test_infra_findings.incognito_tradeoff: Incognito would force re-login (no Mistral session cookies in incognito) — incompatible with auth-cached service runs. Use Disable cache + URL cache-buster instead.
test_infra_findings.646_verdict_correction: #646's claim 'v7 fields didn't apply' was incorrect — that was cached evidence. Re-test discipline with cache-buster is required for any retest where prior verdict was 'fix didn't deploy'.

recommendation_for_dev: BREAKTHROUGH: v7 IS deployed correctly. Engine emits valid 1707B envelope with warning content. The bug is in the SuperJSON meta.values transformer mismatch.

v8 fix candidate (HP-3): inspect engine's meta.values generation logic. Specifically check fields where JSON payload has null but meta.values describes 'undefined' marker. Most likely culprits: messages[0].contentChunks[i]._context, messages[0].agentId, messages[0].agentsApiAgentId, messages[0].createdById, messages[0].nextVersion, messages[0].prevVersion, messages[0].model, messages[0].quotedContent, messages[0].workflowExecution.

Two v8 fix approaches: (a) REMOVE those entries from meta.values so JSON null is passed through as null (simplest), (b) ENSURE JSON payload sets those fields to undefined (omit from JSON entirely) so meta.values markers match.
After v8: expect either bubble visible (HP-3 right) OR still no bubble + new error class (HP-3 wrong, narrow to renderer issue not parser).
Engine team can validate v8 locally by running envelope through actual SuperJSON.deserialize() in unit test (no browser dep) to confirm Unknown error gone.
POSITIVE: All wire-level concerns from v5/v6/v7 series resolved. Only the meta.values↔JSON consistency remains as the final blocker.
notes: Cycle 96 mistral v7 RETEST: cache-buster confirmed v7 IS deployed (status='active', content has warning, size=1707B matches). #646's 'didn't deploy' verdict was wrong — cached v6 state. End-to-end FAIL persists due to identical TRPCClientError 'Unknown error' (all fields undefined). Root cause narrowed to SuperJSON meta.values↔JSON null consistency mismatch (HP-3). v8 fix should remove undefined markers from meta.values for fields where JSON has null. Major diagnostic breakthrough.
